reasoning: To resolve YES, Jan-Krzysztof Duda's rating on the Wikipedia FIDE rankings page on 2026-03-22 must be at least 1% higher than on 2026-03-15. Duda's rating is 2739, so a 1% increase would require gaining over 27 Elo points. This resolves to NO for two conclusive reasons: 1) FIDE officially publishes completely new ratings lists only once a month (on the 1st of each month). The Wikipedia article strictly reflects these official monthly updates, meaning that Wikipedia's reported rating for Duda on March 15 will be exactly the same as on March 22 (the March 1st list). 2) Even analyzing pure chess realities, gaining +27 Elo points in a single week for a 2700+ rated super-GM would require winning around 6-7 consecutive games against similarly rated players, which is exceptionally virtually impossible. Therefore, the chance of this occurring and being recorded on Wikipedia between mid-month dates is negligible.
